author: @Pulmonox
text:
I gave up attempting to separate art from artist a long time ago.

I do not want to give these people even a semblance of my attention, nor do I want to entertain anything they create unless it has actual practical use.

Entertainment media (and 'art' in general) has no practical use, and is easily replaced.

replies.text: That’s so true

replies.author: @andresanguianozuniga6798
replies.text:
Practical use...
Like...
What obeying what you like no matter what?

Because i doubt you say that last utilitarian thing with things YOU LIKE.

I'm not defending Gunn, but i hope you are not the Typical Reactionary who claps like seal when artists "own libs".

replies.author: @Pulmonox
replies.text:
@andresanguianozuniga6798 By 'practical use' I mean an actual tool, or product that has a purpose beyond 'help me forget my life for an hour'.  If the product is good enough I will still buy it if I have a use for it, even if the creator is abhorrent. 

No, I don't toss my money around at just anyone because I might agree with them. They still have to make something I actually have an interest in.  I work too hard for my dollar to throw them away out of spite.  More people need to start being more discerning about how they spend their money.

I'm fine even with spending money on entertainment made by someone who hates me so long as said person doesn't voice their disdain regularly, publicly and doesn't make it evident in their end product.

But the things Gunn has said publicly (and offered no apology) as well as his general attitude have turned me off to any of his future products, regardless as to their individual quality.

I can't even LOOK at Luke Skywalker the same anymore.

replies.author: @PaulusAlone
replies.text:
I think the culture war in itself proves how ART is vital to us all because our art and culture is where we go to relax, enjoy ourselves and also express ourselves authentically!
Unfortunately that escapism and authenticity of expression has been totally influenced (and often infiltrated) by dogmatic social media influencers and progressive political types who see this as a new front in their war of dogma!
It’s what happens when you go to a sports game and see the teams ‘taking the knee’ before it starts.
It just creeps into everything a little bit at a time until you notice that everything in your pop culture that you used to enjoy like games, music, sports and movies is now this horrible one tone one note of funless grey…

text: Realistically, the chances that there would be people making stuff like the rumor is describing is 100% if Superman was real.  But this is a comic book character and not a "deconstruction" of comic books.  If he doesn't play it straight with the character and the plot, I just won't go - and there are millions just like me out there.  Gunn needs to make a Superman movie with Superman saving people, Clark Kent being clumsy and nerdy, Lois being beautiful and driven, and Lex Luthor being evil.  There's the formula, add nothing to it and you have a winner.  But add in politics, other heroes (aside from very brief cameos), real world stuff and you lose the audience.  Lose this movie, Gunn, and your DC aspirations are toast.
